resources.string:
Your dash speaks CFMoto's MotoPlay protocol, which expects the CFMoto app. This app pretends to be that app and feeds it Android Auto instead.

When you tap Connect:

• the phone runs Android Auto against itself and captures what it draws
• that picture is re-encoded for your dash and sent over the bike's Wi-Fi
• the dash shows it. Maps, Waze and Spotify all just work.

First run, once:

1. On the dash, open MotoPlay so the QR appears, then scan it here. It carries the Wi-Fi name, password and the model id that picks your screen. It's saved, so you only do this once.
2. Android Auto must be installed and set up, with "Add new cars to Android Auto" turned on.

After that: ignition on, open the app, done.

Driving the dash: use the handlebar buttons, the knob and D-pad on the Control tab, or just type a destination and let Maps do the rest.

If the dash stays black: close the app, ignition off and on, try again. The dash only gives out one session at a time.
